title: Cestichis cinnabarina
taxonomy: Cestichis cinnabarina is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Cestichis, which is a small group of orchids native to Southeast Asia. Cestichis cinnabarina is further classified as a member of the subtribe Coelogyneinae, a subtribe within the tribe Coelogyneae. This classification helps to group plants with similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships. By classifying Cestichis cinnabarina within the orchid family, scientists can better understand its biology and ecology.

intro: Cestichis cinnabarina, also known as Cinnamon Vine, is a climbing plant native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of C. cinnabarina include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, C. cinnabarina is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
